lesson: Write characterization tests that record current output, bugs included, as the baseline before refactoring, so changes are measured against real behavior, not assumed intent.
Apply Extract Method whenever a comment is needed to explain a code block: the extracted method's name replaces the comment and becomes a seam you can test in isolation.
Find a seam, a point where behavior can change without editing that line, such as a virtual call or a swappable collaborator, to insert a test double around legacy code.
Break a two-module import cycle by extracting the interface both sides actually need into a third module, then point both originals at it instead of each other.
Replace a hard-coded `new ConcreteDependency()` with constructor or parameter injection so a test can substitute a fake without editing the class under test's internals.
Migrate with a Strangler Fig facade: route one call path at a time from the old system to the new implementation, and delete the old path only after traffic fully shifts.
Inline Method to remove indirection that has only one caller and adds no clarity; a seam nobody uses for testing or extension is just a maintenance cost.
For an untestable constructor doing I/O, extract the offending call into a protected method and subclass in the test to override it with a fixture, a stepping stone toward full dependency injection.
After breaking a dependency cycle, add a CI check that walks the module graph and fails the build on any new cross-package cycle, so the fix cannot silently regress.
